nodes.label: Grade Beauty Ladder (C/B/A/S Visual Budget)
nodes.rationale: Grade determines visual richness rather than power — the beauty ladder decouples cosmetic progression from stat progression, keeping the no-model-judgment pillar intact while giving players a visible reward for grade advancement.

nodes.label: Palette Indirection (House Tint + Grade LUT)
nodes.rationale: Sprites store palette indices rather than RGB so one asset serves all grade/House combinations. Color resolves at render time via LUTs, enabling live grade-shift without new art assets.

nodes.label: UsageEvent Normalized Stream Contract
nodes.rationale: Adapters emit one normalized UsageEvent stream so the engine is provider-blind. Format drift in any provider is an adapter patch, never an engine change.

nodes.label: AdapterCheckpoint Incremental Ingestion
nodes.rationale: Providers prune logs (e.g. Claude Code 30-day deletion); keeping per-file offset+mtime checkpoints and reading only new bytes ensures the game's own store is the durable record.

nodes.label: MOLT_CHECKPOINT (5-hour session window close)
nodes.rationale: All evolution, trait, mutation and grade-up events fire only at molt — a single, predictable checkpoint. This makes the cycle deterministic and prevents mid-session state from breaking replay invariants.

nodes.label: Seeded RNG (mulberry32, serializable)
nodes.rationale: A serializable seeded RNG ensures determinism — same saved state + same events + same clock always yield identical results. Math.random() is banned in packages/core by ESLint.

nodes.label: Evolution Branching (data-driven evolvesTo)
nodes.rationale: Branch conditions are data in evolvesTo[], not hardcoded engine logic, enabling species to be added without engine changes (content-as-data pillar).

nodes.label: Diff Renderer (front/back cell buffer, ANSI delta emit)
nodes.rationale: Only emitting ANSI for changed cells with run-coalescing keeps CPU near 0% in idle and respects the <2ms flush budget. An identical re-flush must emit zero bytes (tested).
nodes.norm_label: diff renderer (front/back cell buffer, ansi delta emit)

nodes.label: 4:3 Game Canvas Layout Law
nodes.rationale: Cells are ~1:2 w:h and half-blocks give 2 vertical px per cell, so a 4:3 visual canvas uses an ~8:3 cols:rows grid. Letterboxing uses habitat-tinted gutters so content never intrudes.

nodes.label: Golden Frame Renderer Testing (string buffer snapshots)
nodes.rationale: TUIs are very testable by rendering to a string buffer and snapshot-comparing. Never weaken a golden frame — regenerate only when the visual change is intended and reviewed.
nodes.norm_label: golden frame renderer testing (string buffer snapshots)

nodes.label: Additive-Only Registries (registry-freeze.json)
nodes.rationale: Never removing/renumbering ids means every hash ever shared stays valid forever. Unknown ids become dormant genes rather than errors, keeping old codes alive across all versions.

nodes.label: Spoiler Policy (fusion-pool contents internal only)
nodes.rationale: Discovery is community content. Datamining is inevitable but official docs never spoil — CI fails if pool species names appear in docs/wiki/README.

nodes.label: Release Workflow (tag v* → binaries + GitHub Release)
nodes.norm_label: release workflow (tag v* → binaries + github release)

nodes.label: DNA Fusion, Hash/DNA Code System, and Battle System
nodes.norm_label: dna fusion, hash/dna code system, and battle system

nodes.label: 8 Design Pillars (Idle / No-Judgment / Horizontal / Version-Agnostic / Provider-Agnostic / Social / Offline / Completionist)
nodes.rationale: Normative constraints that govern every feature, mechanic, and content decision. They exist to ensure Token Tamers never spends quota, privileges any provider, or requires mandatory player interaction beyond tt init.
nodes.norm_label: 8 design pillars (idle / no-judgment / horizontal / version-agnostic / provider-agnostic / social / offline / completionist)

nodes.label: Read-Only Observer Principle
nodes.rationale: Provider policies prohibit using subscription credentials in third-party tools. Token Tamers reads local session logs already written to disk — the pet grows because the dev shipped real work, not because a script burned tokens.

nodes.label: DNA Hash Code Format (TT<schema>-<content_min>-<payload>-<sig>)
nodes.rationale: Self-describing schema prefix means old clients parse newer hashes gracefully and every hash ever shared stays valid forever — forward-compatibility is baked into the format.

nodes.label: DNA Fusion System (apply-timing tiers, one application per lifetime)
nodes.norm_label: dna fusion system (apply-timing tiers, one application per lifetime)

nodes.label: Battle System (deterministic f(hashA,hashB,ruleset_version))
nodes.rationale: Pure deterministic function of two hashes and a ruleset version means replays are reproducible forever — no RNG from wall-clock time, no network, no per-session state.

nodes.label: 4-House Type Wheel (Aether > Cipher > Flux > Forge > Aether)
nodes.norm_label: 4-house type wheel (aether > cipher > flux > forge > aether)

nodes.label: Provider-Anonymity Rule (hashes carry gene IDs not provider names)
nodes.rationale: Houses are the public vocabulary in battle/trading. Only Houses show — preserving provider parity so cross-provider battles are House-vs-House not brand-vs-brand.
nodes.norm_label: provider-anonymity rule (hashes carry gene ids not provider names)

nodes.label: Archive (Hall of Fame — best-record per species)
nodes.norm_label: archive (hall of fame — best-record per species)

nodes.label: Horizontal Evolution (equal total stat budget across all final forms)
nodes.rationale: Different builds not better builds — stat distribution flavor differs by House but totals are always equal. This keeps model/provider choice cosmetic and preserves competitive fairness.
nodes.norm_label: horizontal evolution (equal total stat budget across all final forms)

nodes.label: Dormant Genes (unknown model IDs / species → ??? silhouette)
nodes.rationale: Unknown ids become dormant genes rather than errors. This enables version-agnostic parsing: hashes from newer versions are never rejected by older clients.
nodes.norm_label: dormant genes (unknown model ids / species → ??? silhouette)

nodes.label: AI-Native Development Policy
nodes.rationale: Humans own architecture and contracts; CI owns quality and performance; AI writes the code. The feedback loops are mechanical — import-boundary lint, determinism tests, golden frames, fixture suites — not social/process agreements.

nodes.label: CI Gate Suite (zero-network, spoiler, runtime-deps, kebab-case, workflow-pins)
nodes.norm_label: ci gate suite (zero-network, spoiler, runtime-deps, kebab-case, workflow-pins)

nodes.label: Supply-Chain Security (lifecycle scripts blocked, release cooldown, SHA-pinned actions)
nodes.rationale: Defense against npm-worm attacks: lifecycle scripts blocked by pnpm, minimumReleaseAge keeps freshly-poisoned releases from being installed, all GitHub Actions pinned to full commit SHAs.
nodes.norm_label: supply-chain security (lifecycle scripts blocked, release cooldown, sha-pinned actions)

nodes.label: Custom Slim TUI Core (no Ink/OpenTUI framework)
nodes.rationale: Ink adds ~50MB React overhead; OpenTUI requires Bun or Node 26 experimental FFI. A custom core gives zero runtime deps, no native binaries, and works on any Node LTS over SSH.
nodes.norm_label: custom slim tui core (no ink/opentui framework)

nodes.label: Weekly Arc & Weather (Growth/Bloom/Twilight acts + deterministic weather seed)
nodes.norm_label: weekly arc & weather (growth/bloom/twilight acts + deterministic weather seed)

nodes.label: House Wild (unmatched model IDs / neutral / dormant gene)
nodes.norm_label: house wild (unmatched model ids / neutral / dormant gene)

nodes.label: Adapter Fixture Testing (anonymized real log samples)
nodes.norm_label: adapter fixture testing (anonymized real log samples)

nodes.label: Import Boundary Invariant (core imports nothing; tui/adapters import core only)
nodes.rationale: Enforced by ESLint to prevent architectural drift. AI-generated code is prone to adding cross-package imports; mechanical enforcement catches violations immediately in CI.
nodes.norm_label: import boundary invariant (core imports nothing; tui/adapters import core only)

nodes.label: Zero Network Code Invariant
nodes.rationale: No code capable of a network request exists in the repo — ESLint bans network module imports and CI greps every PR. Social features work by humans pasting text codes.

nodes.label: Deterministic Core Invariant (no Date.now/Math.random in packages/core)
nodes.rationale: Time enters as event timestamps and randomness via the seeded RNG. Same state + same events + same clock => identical results forever. Required for cross-version battle replay reproducibility.
nodes.norm_label: deterministic core invariant (no date.now/math.random in packages/core)

nodes.label: Zero Runtime Dependencies Invariant
nodes.rationale: devDependencies only; the tt bundle is self-contained. Reduces supply-chain attack surface and enables zero-network CI trivially.

nodes.label: Mote (Universal Egg — first molt commits House)
nodes.norm_label: mote (universal egg — first molt commits house)

nodes.label: Hybrid Evolution Lines (Mistral: Aether×Flux; Obsidian: Forge×Cipher)
nodes.norm_label: hybrid evolution lines (mistral: aether×flux; obsidian: forge×cipher)

nodes.label: Chimera-Class Multi-Agent Design Statement
nodes.rationale: Polyhost+Switcher → Chimera pattern and its DNA pool is the design's explicit statement that no provider is privileged and that multi-provider workflows produce unique, unreachable content.

nodes.label: Content as Data Principle
nodes.rationale: Species/traits/pools/models.json/rules live in versioned JSON, never hardcoded, so format drift in any content area is a data patch, not an engine change. Enables additive-only evolution.

nodes.label: Lifecycle and Cycle Policies Design Doc
nodes.norm_label: lifecycle and cycle policies design doc

nodes.label: tt init Setup Wizard
nodes.rationale: One-time interactive wizard is the ONLY required interaction ever. Design intent: maximize idle nature by front-loading all setup into one session, so subsequent usage is entirely passive.

nodes.label: Dynamic Cycle Policy (Subscription)
nodes.rationale: For subscription plans (Claude Pro/Max, ChatGPT): infers 5-hour session windows from usage gaps + plan reset schedule. Chosen because subscription users have real windowed limits that naturally segment sessions.

nodes.label: Static Cycle Policy (API/Pay-as-you-go)
nodes.rationale: For API/pay-as-you-go users: fixed 5-hour windows from a configurable anchor. Chosen because API users lack natural session boundaries, so a fixed grid provides consistent molt checkpoints.

nodes.label: Calibration Egg (Cold Start)
nodes.rationale: During the first week without a baseline, the egg plays normally but grades are provisional. Design intent: let new players engage immediately without penalizing them for lacking historical data.

nodes.label: MOLT_CHECKPOINT Abstract Event
nodes.rationale: The ONLY moment a pet can change stage, roll a trait, mutate, or evolve. Design ensures all progression is coupled to real work sessions, never to calendar time alone.

nodes.label: REBIRTH Abstract Event
nodes.rationale: Week boundary: ascension, Archive record write, inheritance roll, new egg. Rebirth never evolves the pet — final form is set by the last molt. Separates archiving/reset from evolution to keep the two distinct.

nodes.label: Weekly Three-Act Arc (Growth/Bloom/Twilight)
nodes.rationale: Structures the week into three emotional beats: Growth (days 1-3), Bloom (days 4-6, guaranteed rare roll), Twilight (final 24h, legacy crystallizes). Design gives pacing and predictable high points to the idle cycle.

nodes.label: Weekly Weather (Deterministic Seed)
nodes.rationale: Deterministic seed from ISO week number biases trait rates. Shared by all players globally with no server. Provides communal novelty and variety without any network requirement.

nodes.label: Multi-Provider Normalization Rule
nodes.rationale: All adapters feed ONE pet; essence normalized per-adapter against its own baseline then summed. Adding a second agent never inflates power, only diversifies diet. Prevents multi-provider advantage and enforces no-model-judgment pillar.

nodes.label: Dormancy (Week of Zero Usage)
nodes.rationale: Zero usage = pet goes dormant (cocoon, not death). Design intent: never punish players for breaks — re-entry is always warm, dormancy is itself an achievement.

nodes.label: Year-One Retention Plan
nodes.rationale: Quarterly content packs, monthly weather events, Team Leagues, Legacy milestones, and Drifter DNA to sustain engagement beyond the initial 4-6 month window. Designed to extend retention to 12 months without network requirements.

nodes.label: Drifter DNA (Solo-Dev Bridge)
nodes.rationale: Generated locally by tt dna drifter from deterministic calendar seed. Every machine on the same month produces the identical code, fully offline — solo players get fusion content without network and without publishing. Bridges the solo-dev cliff where no colleagues means no fusion third.

nodes.label: Team Leagues (Opt-In Social)
nodes.rationale: Weekly standings computed locally from hashes colleagues paste to each other (tt league import <codes>). Any human channel works. The game never transmits or receives anything — purely local computation from shared codes.

nodes.label: Visual Design, Habitats & Achievements Design Doc
nodes.norm_label: visual design, habitats & achievements design doc

nodes.label: Per-Stage Sprite Size Law (2026-06-13 Decision Record)
nodes.rationale: Supersedes 48-64px sizing. Small high-density characters at ~1/3 the old footprint: egg=10, sprite=12, rookie=14, evolved=16, prime=18, apex=20 (square px). At these sizes dithered gradients read as noise, so flat tones with 2-3px dither seams are mandated. Content-pack test enforces exact square dimensions as a hard law.
nodes.norm_label: per-stage sprite size law (2026-06-13 decision record)

nodes.label: Grade Beauty Ladder (C/B/A/S Visual Tiers)
nodes.rationale: Grade determines visual richness, not just hue. C=flat 4-color palette; B=8-color+blink; A=16-color+dithered gradient; S=24-bit gradients+animated shimmer+particle aura+glow. Richness is the reward for grade progression, making visual beauty a gameplay outcome.

nodes.label: Gradeshift LUT Hot-Swap
nodes.rationale: On Gradeshift, renderer hot-swaps grade LUT — same sprite asset instantly re-renders richer, with burst cutscene. Mid-week jackpot moment worth screenshotting. Design: one asset covers all grades, reducing content size while making grade progression visually dramatic.

nodes.label: Palette Indirection (Index-not-RGB)
nodes.rationale: Sprite assets store palette indices, never RGB. Grade + House select the LUT at render time — one asset, many beauty levels. Enables grade beauty ladder and House tints from a single content asset.

nodes.label: Half-Block Pixel Rendering (▀ cells)
nodes.rationale: ▀ cells: fg=top pixel, bg=bottom pixel → 2 vertical pixels per cell. A 32×32-pixel pet fits in 32×16 cells and reads as true colorful pixel art (chafa-style). Maximum visual resolution within terminal character-cell constraints.

nodes.label: Diff Renderer (Front/Back Buffer)
nodes.rationale: Front/back buffers; emit ANSI only for changed cells. Idle pet redraws ~30 cells/frame, never the full screen. Keeps CPU at ~1-2% at 30fps by making the most common case (static background) essentially free.

nodes.label: 30fps Slim-First + Adaptive Down Policy
nodes.rationale: 30fps default; adaptive down to 15fps if terminal can't drain; idle scenes drop to ~10fps event-driven; 60fps opt-in only. Beauty degrades before correctness. Balances smooth animation with <1% CPU budget.

nodes.label: Habitats (Background Scenes)
nodes.rationale: Strictly cosmetic (zero stat/grade/rarity influence). Unlock through achievements; day/night tint follows real clock; weekly weather seed adds ambient effects. Depth layer that rewards progression without affecting balance.

nodes.label: Trinkets (Toys & Objects)
nodes.rationale: Small sprites in anchor slots; pet idle animations interact with them based on traits. Purely cosmetic — influence which idles play, never stats. Trait-flavored idle variety rewards trait diversity without gameplay advantage.

nodes.label: Achievements Registry
nodes.rationale: Declarative {id, name, condition, reward} schema in achievements.json. Every achievement grants something tangible (habitat/trinket/title/dex_flair). Evaluated locally at molt/rebirth. No network, purchases, or model-specific requirements.

nodes.label: Completion Meter (tt complete)
nodes.rationale: Single number: weighted union of Dex + achievements + habitats + trinkets. The endgame North Star driving to 100%. Displayed in the TUI bottom menu bar. Weighting: 40/40/10/10.

nodes.label: Degradation Ladder (Truecolor → ASCII)
nodes.rationale: 4 levels: truecolor → 256-color quantized → 8-color+badges → pure ASCII (--no-color). Beauty scales down; information never disappears. Ensures game works in any terminal environment.

nodes.label: Adapter → UsageEvent → Core → Store → TUI Pipeline
nodes.norm_label: adapter → usageevent → core → store → tui pipeline

nodes.label: Core Engine Determinism Guarantee
nodes.rationale: Pure, deterministic — no I/O, no wall clock, no Math.random. Same state + same events + same clock = identical results forever. Enables hash sharing: DNA codes computed anywhere produce identical game-state reconstructions.

nodes.label: Houses (Aether/Cipher/Flux/Forge/Wild)
nodes.rationale: Identity and cosmetics only; equal stat budgets. Model-ID family maps to House; stat lean redistributes equal budget. 'Lean' never makes one House stronger — this enforces the no-model-judgment pillar at identity level.

nodes.label: Traits (Marathoner/Sprinter/Polyglot/etc.)
nodes.rationale: Rolled once per molt, triggered by work patterns never by model choice or volume. Up to ~5 slots. Pattern evolutions (Vigil, Tempest, Prism, Chimera) lock at the week's final molt from trait combinations.

nodes.label: Grade Roll System (C→B→A→S, Monotonic)
nodes.rationale: C→B 25%, B→A 10%, A→S 3% base; activity modifier ×0.5–2.0; A→S hard cap ~6%. Monotonic — never goes down. No pity guarantee. Odds always shown in UI. Transparent probabilistic system prevents loss-aversion while maintaining long-term chase.

nodes.label: Activity Modifier (Model-Neutral Molt Signals)
nodes.rationale: From consistency vs own baseline, rhythm quality, trait synergy, diversity. Token volume and model choice never enter it. All normalization is relative to the player's own history, not absolute volume.

nodes.label: Archive (Best-Per-Species Record)
nodes.rationale: One best-record slot per species: final grade + final stats, overwritten only if strictly better (grade first, total stats tiebreak). Records persist across rebirths, forming the permanent completionist record.

nodes.label: The Three Pledges (Read-Only/Offline/No-Model-Judgment)
nodes.rationale: Core trust contract: (1) never calls AI API/spends tokens, (2) zero network code enforced by CI, (3) model choice flavors identity only, progression normalized to own baseline. These are user-facing promises underpinned by CI invariants.

nodes.label: allowBuilds Allowlist (esbuild only)
nodes.rationale: Dependency lifecycle scripts blocked by default; esbuild is the ONLY approved exception. Defense against npm-worm style supply-chain attacks where malicious packages execute install scripts.

nodes.label: minimumReleaseAge: 4320 min (3-day Cooldown)
nodes.rationale: Refuse to install package versions younger than 3 days. Compromised releases are usually yanked within hours/days; the cooldown means a freshly-published trojan version never reaches the project.

nodes.label: README Shell GIF (Aurelion S-Grade Apex Screenshot)
nodes.norm_label: readme shell gif (aurelion s-grade apex screenshot)
